identitySentence: The Law Office of David S Bouschor II PC is a Denton family law firm serving the area since 2000 with a focus on divorce, child custody, probate, and guardianship matters.

concept.headline: Denton Family Law Guidance for Divorce, Custody, and Everything That Follows
concept.subheadline: Family legal problems are personal, and the uncertainty is often the hardest part. A consultation with the Law Office of David S. Bouschor, II, P.C. is a calm, honest conversation about your situation and the realistic options in front of you.
credibilityPoints: Board Certified in Family Law by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization since 1995
Serving Denton and the surrounding areas since 2000
Founding attorney David S. Bouschor II practicing law since 1991

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: The firm handles the full range of family law matters, including divorce, collaborative divorce, high-asset divorce, child custody, child support, spousal maintenance, modifications, enforcements, paternity, grandparents' rights, and premarital agreements. It also practices in mediation, probate, probate litigation, and guardianship, so related estate and family concerns can be addressed under one roof.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: David S. Bouschor II opened the firm in Denton in January 2000, and it has served Denton and the surrounding areas ever since. The office is located at 217 East Oak Street, Denton, Texas 76201, with a practice focused on families across Denton County.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Your consultation is a straightforward conversation about your situation, your goals, and the paths realistically available to you under Texas law. Expect the firm to ask about your family circumstances, finances, and any existing court orders or agreements so the guidance you receive applies to your actual case, not a generic one.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

To the Law Office of David S. Bouschor, II, P.C.,

Your firm has served Denton and the surrounding areas since 2000, and David S. Bouschor II has been Board Certified in Family Law by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization since 1995. A record like that means potential clients come to you with serious, time-sensitive problems, and family law inquiries rarely arrive during business hours.

Your website already does a great deal right: deep practice-area content, extensive trust signals, and a well-built contact form. What no static site can do is answer back. When someone submits a custody question at 9 p.m., they wait until the office opens, and many quietly keep searching in the meantime. And because appointments are only booked by phone or form, every inquiry still requires a round of back-and-forth before a consultation is actually on the calendar.

That is the gap our Lead Capturer fills. It is a single, self-contained lead-capture page built specifically for your firm, carrying your own identity: "The Law Office of David S Bouschor II PC is a Denton family law firm serving the area since 2000 with a focus on divorce, child custody, probate, and guardianship matters." It includes a custom contact form written around your actual practice, from divorce, collaborative divorce, and child custody through probate and guardianship.

Here is what happens when someone reaches out:

1. Their inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox, with every form field included and a subject line that identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
2. The potential client automatically receives a topical response specific to their message, with no staff action required.
3. That response presents your genuinely available consultation times.
4. A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so the consultations on your calendar are committed ones.

The classification behind those responses is deterministic and rule-based, so every reply is predictable, on-topic, and reviewable by you before anything goes live.

We offer a 15-day proof period. Proof matters more than promises: run the complete experience yourselves, from first form submission to a confirmed appointment, and judge the results on what you actually observe. A single family law consultation that would otherwise have slipped away can cover the cost of the Lead Capturer before you ever have to.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject identifies the firm and likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email without staff action.
9. Confirm the customer receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual inquiry topic.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us.

We would welcome the chance to show you the demo page built for your firm and walk through it together.

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
